# Graduated Review Authority

> Graduated Review Authority (GRA) is a governance model for AI-native software delivery. It matches how much review a change needs to the risk the change carries and the strength of the evidence that it is correct, so trivial changes move quickly and risky ones still receive human judgment.

GRA replaces a single fixed review path with a graduated one. Review authority is earned from objective evidence rather than assumed from who or what wrote the change: low-risk, well-validated work can move with less human review, while high-risk work always receives human judgment. The published page at https://gra.dev is the authoritative source for the concept.

The model rests on three sources of evidence (deterministic gates, agent review, and human review) and a dual-complexity sizing method that estimates review load and predicts the resulting review level: auto-eligible, agent review, or human review.

## Docs

- [Graduated Review Authority: full explainer in Markdown](https://gra.dev/index.html.md): The complete page content as clean Markdown, covering all ten sections from what it is through the wider context.
- [Graduated Review Authority: full explainer](https://gra.dev/): The same content as the published HTML page.
- [Project overview in Markdown](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/joshuamil/gra.dev/main/README.md): A concise Markdown summary of the model.
